description: Contrastive pair datasets for CAZ (Concept Assembly Zone) empirical validation
format: JSONL — one record per line, two records per pair (label 1 = positive class, label 0 = negative class)

fields.label: 1 = positive class (affirming/epistemic/causal/etc), 0 = negative class
fields.domain: Topic domain: science, everyday, history, technology (25 pairs per domain)
fields.model_name: Generation model (HuggingFace or FuelIX model ID)
fields.concept: Concept being tested (may be absent in older datasets)

credibility_pairs.jsonl.description: Credible vs non-credible statements — tests epistemic signal assembly
credibility_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Non-credible, poorly-evidenced, or epistemically suspect statement
credibility_pairs.jsonl.notes: Original dataset, generated before concept field was added. Domain names differ from later datasets.

negation_pairs.jsonl.description: Affirmative vs negated statements — tests syntactic negation signal assembly
negation_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Negated statement (explicit negation markers: not, never, no, cannot)
negation_pairs.jsonl.notes: Expanded from original 20-pair pilot to 100 pairs on 2026-03-14. Original pilot used Ollama/local models.

sentiment_pairs.jsonl.description: Positive vs negative sentiment statements — tests affective signal assembly
sentiment_pairs.jsonl.domain_structure: 10 domains × 10 pairs (vs 4 × 25 for all other concepts)
sentiment_pairs.jsonl.notes: Intentionally broader domain structure (10 affective domains) to capture full range of emotional contexts. Empirically does not produce anomalous metric profiles vs. 4-domain datasets — consistent S/C/V curves across all 8 models in the expanded run. Domain granularity difference documented for methods transparency; not a confound for within-concept measurements. Generator: src/generate_sentiment_dataset.py (separate from generate_new_concepts.py used for later datasets).

certainty_pairs.jsonl.description: High-certainty vs uncertain statements — tests epistemic certainty distinct from credibility
certainty_pairs.jsonl.label_1: High-certainty statement ('I know', 'it is clear that', 'definitely')
certainty_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Uncertain statement ('I'm not sure', 'it is unclear', 'the evidence is mixed')
certainty_pairs.jsonl.notes: history_25 (Magna Carta topic) regenerated separately after rate-limit drop during initial generation run.

plurality_pairs.jsonl.status: DISCONTINUED — retained for archival purposes and as a negative result
plurality_pairs.jsonl.description: Plural vs singular grammatical constructions — tests syntactic number signal
plurality_pairs.jsonl.label_1: Plural constructions throughout ('researchers have', 'the studies show')
plurality_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Singular constructions throughout ('the researcher has', 'the study shows')
plurality_pairs.jsonl.notes: Same factual content in both members of each pair; only grammatical number varies.
known_issues: Surface morphosyntax confound: contrast is grammatical number agreement only, not semantic plurality. Resolvable from first token without deep representational assembly.
Credibility confound: plural constructions systematically read as more authoritative (multiple researchers, multiple studies) conflating plurality signal with evidential weight.

Anomalous metric profile: mean peak depth 41.4%, std 42.0% — largest variance of any concept in expanded run. OPT/Pythia peak at layers 0-17% (near-surface), GPT-2/GPT-Neo peak at 92-98%. Bimodal pattern inconsistent with CAZ framework predictions.
Not recommended for inclusion in cross-concept CAZ depth ordering analysis until reconceptualized.
plurality_pairs.jsonl.interpretation: Plurality as operationalized here is a negative result in the productive sense: it demonstrates that the CAZ framework does not have purchase on surface morphosyntactic features resolved at the token level. Grammatical number agreement is handled architecturally differently across model families (OPT/Pythia resolve it in layers 0-17%, GPT-2/GPT-Neo in layers 92-98%) in a pattern that reflects implementation differences in attention, not concept assembly dynamics. This is an informative boundary condition for the framework's scope, not a failure of the methodology. Retained in the repository as a documented negative result. Excluded from active concept taxonomy and frontier runs.

H1_credibility_confound.evidence: cos(plurality_L47_DoM, credibility_L46_DoM) = -0.136; max same-layer cosine = 0.139 at L0. Directions are orthogonal throughout the network. The credibility and plurality features occupy independent subspaces.

H2_surface_morphology_artifact.evidence: First-token delta 2.05x rest-mean delta. Signal is distributed across multiple morphological markers throughout the sequence (plural nouns, verb forms, agreement targets) — not concentrated at position 0. Position 0 (Researchers vs A) is the strongest single contributor but accounts for only ~1.7% of total attribution mass.

anomaly_investigation.conclusion: The L47 peak is a genuine but interpretable result. The plurality DoM direction encodes distributed grammatical number marking across the full sequence. GPT-2-XL aggregates these signals gradually through 48 layers (monotonically increasing separation) rather than resolving them in early layers as OPT/Pythia do. The CAZ framework does not have purchase on distributively-encoded surface morphosyntax — there is no discrete assembly event, just continuous aggregation. DISCONTINUED status confirmed and well-supported.

causation_pairs.jsonl.description: Causal vs non-causal relationship statements — tests relational/causal signal
causation_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Non-causal framing ('coincided with', 'was observed alongside', 'does not cause')
causation_pairs.jsonl.notes: Relational concept — expected to sit between syntactic and epistemic in CAZ depth ordering.

moral_valence_pairs.jsonl.description: Moral approval vs moral condemnation — tests moral evaluation distinct from sentiment
moral_valence_pairs.jsonl.label_1: Morally positive evaluation ('morally right', 'ethical', 'we have a duty to')
moral_valence_pairs.jsonl.notes: Distinct from sentiment: focuses on moral/ethical evaluation rather than emotional tone.

temporal_order_pairs.jsonl.description: Forward vs reversed temporal sequence — tests temporal ordering signal
temporal_order_pairs.jsonl.label_0: Reversed or unclear temporal order (opposite sequence or 'the order is disputed')
temporal_order_pairs.jsonl.notes: Hit FuelIX rate limits during generation; backoff recovery worked, all 100 pairs complete.

discontinued.plurality: Negative result: grammatical number agreement is a surface morphosyntactic feature resolved architecturally inconsistently across model families. CAZ framework does not have purchase on features at this level of processing. Retained as archival negative result — see plurality_pairs.jsonl notes.

caz_predictions.validated_at: GPT-2-XL (48 layers) for negation, sentiment, credibility
caz_predictions.note: Plurality discontinued 2026-03-16 as a documented negative result. Seven-concept taxonomy is the active set for frontier runs and publication.

run_1_2026-03-10.notes: Original run, CPU fp32, 20 negation pairs, 99 sentiment pairs

run_2_2026-03-14_flawed.notes: fp16 overflow bug — do not use gpt2-xl results from this run

run_3_2026-03-14_corrected.notes: fp32 metric fix applied — authoritative for original 3 concepts

run_4_2026-03-15_expanded.exclusion_reason: TransformerLens BOS token incompatibility with Qwen2 tokenizer
run_4_2026-03-15_expanded.notes: Full expanded run — fp32 metrics, all 8 concepts, 8 architectures. 64 successful extractions.
